description_notes: Campervan HQ price basis from user-provided listing: Lonwood Madera Topseal, Oregano, 72 in wide, sold by the linear foot at $44.73/lf. Qty 8 lf = 48 sq ft for one-piece truck-bed floor cut; no seam materials included in this baseline.

Truck-bed floor is a horizontal vehicle-floor install over birch plywood with the top bond face left untreated to match Lonseal's Vehicle Installation Guide requirement that plywood panels be untreated. Use a full spread of #650 with a 1/16 x 1/32 x 1/32 in U-notch trowel. Half-gallon is sufficient for the current 48 sq ft plan: Lonseal checklist spread-rate table lists #650 at approx. 170-225 sq ft/gal, so 0.5 gal covers about 85-112 sq ft. Do a bond test if installing outside the recommended 65-85 F acclimation/install range. If flooring is wrapped onto wheel wells or other non-horizontal surfaces later, add #400 contact adhesive separately.

Lonseal vehicle guide calls for a 100 lb three-section roller, but specifically allows a smaller laminate-style roller when vehicle access makes a full floor roller impractical. For a truck bed camper build, plan on a compact segmented roller or equivalent instead of a full-size commercial roller.
